case.title: BALTIMORE & OHIO RAILROAD CO. v. UNITED STATES

opinion.h: BALTIMORE & OHIO RAILROAD CO. v. UNITED STATES, 385 U.S. 3 (1966)
BALTIMORE & OHIO RAILROAD CO. ET AL. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 104. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: Samuel P. Delisi and Edward A. Kaier for appellants.

Solicitor General Marshall, Assistant Attorney General Turner, Howard E. Shapiro, Charles L. Marinaccio and Robert W. Ginnane for the United States et al. James M. Carter for appellee Erie-Lackawanna Railroad Co.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
opinion.h: BUCHANAN v. RHODES, GOVERNOR OF OHIO, 385 U.S. 3 (1966)
opinion.p: 385 U.S. 3 BUCHANAN ET AL. v. RHODES, GOVERNOR OF OHIO, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF OHIO. No. 115. Decided October 10, 1966.
William B. Saxbe, Attorney General of Ohio, and Gerald A. Donahue, First Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction.
